explanation: Optimist outperformed Pessimist across all criteria through stronger evidence (specific programs like Germany’s Industry 4.0, IRENA’s renewable job projections), clearer argument structure, and persuasive emphasis on historical parallels and global reskilling efforts. Pessimist’s arguments were well-supported by displacement data but lacked solutions, weakening persuasiveness. Both used similar evidence quality, but Optimist’s broader industry scope and policy examples gave them an edge.

factcheck_flags.claim: LinkedIn 2022 report showing 60% of AI jobs remain unfilled
factcheck_flags.issue: Unverifiable – no specific LinkedIn 2022 report cited

factcheck_flags.claim: Germany’s ‘Industry 4.0’ policies reduced structural unemployment by 22% [BIS 2021]
factcheck_flags.issue: Unverifiable – no BIS 2021 report found confirming this exact claim
